caveats: a: The class combines materially different service models.
a: Feature usefulness does not equal automatable labor hours.
rho: Survey sampling does not establish national adoption.
rho: Benefits may appear as quality or throughput rather than labor reduction.
e: Residual-code assignment depends on primary revenue.
e: No direct externalization measure was located.
s5: Exit intention need not produce an outside sale.
s5: Aftermarket deal reports cover a wider market.
q: Value may appear as capacity or quality.
q: Capture differs across retail and fleet work.
d5: Vehicle age is exposure rather than direct demand.
d5: The occupation covers neighboring automotive activities.
o: Remote diagnosis may reduce stand-alone visits.
o: Automated inspection lanes can reduce labor without removing the operator.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Local vehicle owners and fleets buy episodic inspections, diagnostics, tire repairs, fluid services, and protective treatments through per-job, menu-priced, or labor-plus-materials charges.
lens.excluded_activities: Mechanical and electrical repair; body, paint, interior, and glass work; car washes; new-parts retailing; vehicle dealers; and captive manufacturer or fleet service operations.
lens.included_activities: Independent automotive air-conditioning service, tire repair, lubrication, safety and emissions inspection, diagnostic service without repair, rustproofing, undercoating, spray-on bed liners, and other automotive maintenance not classified elsewhere.

a.bridge: The source population is the broader automotive repair workforce and surveyed repair shops. Administrative, sales, scheduling, estimating, inspection-documentation, and diagnostic tasks are translated into the labor share technically addressable in this residual class. Broader occupational data include less-addressable physical mechanics and can bias downward; an industry survey may overrepresent digital shops and bias upward.
caveats: The class combines materially different service models.
Feature usefulness does not equal automatable labor hours.
a.change_evidence: A representative 811198 time-and-motion study separating physical service from diagnosis, quoting, documentation, and customer workflow would materially change the estimate.
a.rationale: Physical vehicle handling dominates, but customer workflow, inspection records, estimates, scheduling, and portions of diagnosis are amenable to current AI.

d5.bridge: The source population is the U.S. light-vehicle fleet and broader automotive technician occupation. Vehicle age, fleet growth, and occupational demand are translated into five-year real demand for miscellaneous repair. The evidence includes adjacent mechanical and parts activity and can bias upward; electric-vehicle maintenance differences also weaken historical extrapolation.
caveats: Vehicle age is exposure rather than direct demand.
The occupation covers neighboring automotive activities.
d5.change_evidence: Inflation-adjusted 811198 receipts by service line and powertrain, paired with establishment dynamics, would materially change the value.
d5.rationale: An older fleet and longer retention support inspections, tires, diagnostics, lubrication, and protective service, while electric vehicles, improved durability, and consumer deferral temper growth.

caveats: Residual-code assignment depends on primary revenue.
No direct externalization measure was located.
e.change_evidence: Microdata linking customer type, ownership, primary NAICS, and outsourced automotive service spending would materially change the estimate.
e.rationale: Most included services can be supplied by local independent specialists. Dealer work, captive fleet operations, mobile or owner-performed service, and neighboring automotive classifications reduce the external share.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 811198 ($1,657,824,342) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 811198 ($3,680,450,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[81]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $162,335 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 811198); class <5: 3,710 firms, avg revenue/firm $308,519 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $48,283 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 682 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,117,236 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $174,847 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 180 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,157,256 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $337,610 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 66 firms, avg revenue/firm $4,463,818 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $698,588 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 16 firms, avg revenue/firm $41,880,500 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $6,554,298 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 16 firms, avg revenue/firm $26,305,250 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $4,116,772 -> IN $1-10M band; n_band = 32

caveats: Remote diagnosis may reduce stand-alone visits.
Automated inspection lanes can reduce labor without removing the operator.
o.change_evidence: Scaled autonomous inspection, robotic tire or fluid service, or remote diagnostics displacing physical service would materially change the value.
o.rationale: Included services require a vehicle, physical tools, local bay or mobile access, technician accountability, and often regulated inspection. AI changes workflow but rarely removes the accountable operator.

caveats: Value may appear as capacity or quality.
Capture differs across retail and fleet work.
q.change_evidence: Matched evidence on AI labor savings, gross margin, pricing, bay throughput, and retention would materially change the estimate.
q.rationale: Menu prices, flat-rate jobs, and quoted treatments permit capture through throughput and stable pricing. Local competition, fleet purchasing, comparison shopping, and customer approval pass some gains through.

rho.bridge: The source population is repair-shop respondents evaluating AI scheduling, estimate generation, digital inspection, and customer service. Reported usefulness is mapped to feasible 2026 realization. Respondent enthusiasm can exceed sustained deployment and bias upward, while integration and technician-adoption friction reduce realized use.
caveats: Survey sampling does not establish national adoption.
Benefits may appear as quality or throughput rather than labor reduction.
rho.change_evidence: Vendor-neutral evidence on active use, retained labor savings, rework, and paid penetration among 811198 shops would materially change the value.
rho.rationale: Commercially available tools target frequent shop workflows, but fragmented systems, uneven data, technician trust, and liability prevent full realization.

s5.bridge: The source population is U.S. small-business owners reporting near-term exits, supplemented by automotive aftermarket transaction commentary. The national anchor is adjusted for a fragmented market with active consolidators. Cross-industry respondents create unknown-direction bias, while transaction commentary emphasizes visible deals and can bias upward.
caveats: Exit intention need not produce an outside sale.
Aftermarket deal reports cover a wider market.
s5.change_evidence: Owner-age, succession, closure, and transaction data specifically for 811198 would materially change the value.
s5.rationale: Succession creates recurring seller supply and aftermarket buyers consolidate fragmented segments, though many shops are too small or owner-dependent to transact.

principal_driver: Persistent physical service demand from an aging fleet combined with digitally improvable workflow.
principal_weakness: Residual-category heterogeneity and weak direct evidence on economics and AI realization.

run_meta.title: All Other Automotive Repair and Maintenance
